sub-items.label: Features
sub-items.text01:
• NLOS anti-armour missile matching dimensions of standard 120mm shells.

• Integrates with existing weapon and ammunition stowage of in-service MBTs.

• Cost-effective precision missile designed for mass production. 

• Maximises opportunities to destroy enemy platforms whilst minimising risk of return fire, enhancing lethality and increasing survivability.

• Passive seeker enables missile to defeat laser-activated APS.

• Top-attack targets weak-points of enemy vehicles (e.g. turret, engines).

• Fire-and-forget engagement ensures minimal cognitive burden for crew and enables tank to immediately manoeuvre whilst missile is in-flight. 

• Revolutionises combat capabilities of MBTs with no change to silhouette.

sub-items.label: More details
sub-items.text01:
SL ASRAAM is a simple, intuitive, low cost system that needs light logistics support and training.

SL ASRAAM is capable of defeating a broad range of crewed and un-crewed air vehicles, including rotary, fixed wing and missile threats at ranges up to three times greater than typical MANPADS.

Once locked on to its target the ASRAAM missile flies at supersonic speed to intercept. 

The missile guidance on to the selected target is completely automatic with no further input from the operator or weapon system equipment needed. 

It has outstanding resistance to counter measures such as flares launched by aircraft. ASRAAM is immune to GNSS jamming since it uses inertial navigation.

Text
sub-items.text01:
Various sequences of engagement against UAV in before or during engagement of moving MBT by AKERON MP or AKERON LP.
6 autonomous sequences

sub-items.label: More Details
sub-items.text01:
• SEQUENCE 1 & 2: Small UAV are intercepted by HTK drone, Laser or jamming. Engagement of ennemies tanks by AKERON MP.

• SEQUENCE 3: Blocking action of an AKERON MP platoon on vehicle protected by Mistral launchers. Destruction of a tactical UAV by a Mistral just before engagement of 12 MBT by the AKERON MP platoon. Enemy MBT are plotted by a NX70 drone and target data are sent through the LynkEUs network to the different AKERON MP teams. AKERON MP is firing from vehicle, from a house and in openfield.

• SEQUENCE 4: Bridge defended by AKERON MP on UGV is under attack by a swarm of drone detected by radar whereas a patrol of MI35 helicopters is plotted by the Lic²orne of a Mistral platoon. Swarm is engaged and distroyed by a HPM and MI35 are engaged and distroyed by Mistral.
During the engagement of a MBT company by AKERON MP (part3) second echelon is engaged by a patrol of tiger equipped with AKERON LP and Mistral. During the engagement of MBT by Tiger a patrol of ennemi helicopter is detected by a mistral platoon Lic²orne, one of the tiger face the enemy helicopter and engaged the distroyed them by Mistral.

• SEQUENCE 5: To cover ENY frontal engagement by infantry and anti-tank missiles, a pair of Tiger attack helicopters infiltrate the ENY disposal to engage, in the depth, reinforcement and second echelon MBT parties. During MBT engagement with AKERON LP, friend local air defence assets detect and warn the Tiger patrol that a pair of ENY helicopter is flying right to its location. Whereas one Tiger goes on engaging MBT with AKERON LP, the other Tiger, equipped with Air to Air MISTRAL ATAM system, faces and destroys ENY air threat.

sub-items.label: Text
sub-items.text01: Digital Customer Support : Concept of industrial cooperation, leveraging the interoperability of technical solutions provided by both MBDA and ST Engineering.

sub-items.label: More Details
sub-items.text01:
This video exhibits two complementary scenarios of VL Mica Firing Installation failure at sea resolved using cooperative technologies from both MBDA and ST Engineering, demonstrating a concept of a joint remote assistance initiative leveraging secure digital cooperation guaranteeing Customer’s sovereignty  at any time without compromising efficiency
 
In the first scenario, a fault in an electrical cabinet is identified and solved only by national personnel using technologies provided by both MBDA and ST Engineering. Using an AR-Assisted Remote Maintenance tool powered by ST Engineering technology, and based on MBDA datasets coming from the maintenance tablet, the operator is able to fix the failure under supervision from ST Engineering specialists.

In the second scenario, the failure resides in the fire control cabinet, but is not easily identified by personnel as it may be pretty rare. As MBDA FI Specialist intervention is required, a communication canal is opened by ST Engineering to allow for remote failure identification. Once the failure is identified, we demonstrate the ability of the Customer to fully control what MBDA personnel can see or hear in order to preserve full sovereignty of information exchanged with the cooperative technologies.
 
This second scenario also demonstrates the use of RFID technology in order to follow up with applied configuration management on-board, using the MBDA Tablet.

sub-items.label: Text
sub-items.text01: Illustration of the concept of Effect Management through two distinct phases (defensive and offensive) of Naval Warfare

sub-items.label: More Details
sub-items.text01:
The scenario demonstrates how missiles and weapon systems will take part to defensive and offensive operations. This includes:

• Connections between effectors and platforms and between effectors
• Collaborative actions between effectors with different payloads and/or Remote Carriers
• Cooperative engagement plan elaborated by  multiple C2 Systems
• Artificial Intelligence at C2 and effectors for an optimized and timely response against the threats

sub-items.label: More Details
sub-items.text01:
• PHASE 1: 6 NGF cross the frontier, with a complex barrier composed of multiple ground to air threats. Demonstration of different RC's uses,  swarm composed of Smartcruiser and RC vs SAM and Hard kill self protection missile.

• PHASE 2: air to air engagement. Demonstration of RC use in air-air, retargeting of BVRAAM and hard kill self protection missile.

• PHASE 3: airbase targets engagement. Demonstration of RC use, swarm composed of Smartcruiser and RC vs SAM, swarm of SmartGlider vs multiple targets.

sub-items.label: Text
sub-items.text01:
Within the 2040 timeframe, this scenario explores various promising concepts adressing the challenges of high intensity engagements at sea and from the sea. Based on a CVBG deployment off the coast of an hostile country.
4 autonomous sequences

sub-items.label: More Details
sub-items.text01:
• PHASE 1: CVBG defense against a ASBM attacks (Aster B1 NT engagement on remote and Force cooperative engagement). 

• PHASE 2: retaliatory strike against NMI ASCM coastal  and ASBM batteries; this phase shows the use of Surface and subsurface launched Remote Carriers (RCs) for various missions (ISR, ECM, C2, BDA) as well as the combination of NCM, Supoersonic and subsonic FCASW to neuralize air defences and the Missile batteries. 

• PHASE 3: shows a surface engagement between a hostile task group and the CVBG with first the defense against a massive ASCM attack (with hypersonic, supersonic and subsonic missiles) involving B1NT Engagement on remote, B1NT Force cooperative Engagement, VL MICA NG, Sea Ceptor, TAZ (active decoy launcher) and laser engagements for self defence, second the surface engagement of the NMI task group by a search attack group (SAG) of 2 FDI supported by MPA (RC for ISR, coordinated engagement of FCASW supersonic and subsonic as well as RC (ECM, C2, ISR and BDA). 

• PHASE 4: engagement by sub launched air defense missiles of 2 ASW helicopters harassing a friendly SSN. The fourth phase shows a deep stike coordinated engagement against a well protected HVU target with the combination of RCs, FCASW supersonic and subsonic and NCM launched from different platforms (Surface, Subsurface and Air)

• SEQUENCE 2: AKERON MP engagement from RIB against jetski,

sub-items.label: Text
sub-items.text01: Protection of a  large offshore area against various threats: massive air raids, attack of mini UAVs, swarm surface atttack by boats.

sub-items.label: More Details
sub-items.text01:
• PHASE 1: deterrence capability offered by Antiship Coastal Batteries quickly deployed on prepared firing pads and remotely controled from a Naval Operations Center. 

• PHASE 2: Based on a realistic attack ("iranian" attacks against Saudi Oil fields) various solutions to counter a massive air raids (of CM and Drones) combining either VL MICA and SIMBAD RC or CAMM and SIMBAD RC  on barges, locally (TOC) or Remotely (NOC) controled. 

• PHASE 3: CUAS capability being integrated into the Global Air Defense System demonstrated in phase 2. Facing the attack of 3 mini UAV launched from a Dow, the scenario shows the CUAS detection and engagement through the view of different components  (MHR Radar, Majes Jammer, Command Vehicle, HTK Drones, HPM)

• PHASE 4: defense against a swarm surface attack by 10 "pasdaran" boats launched from mother ships ; it includes Multiple Brimstone engagements from a Typhoon, Surface engagement by SIMABD RC on barges, and neutralisation of motherships by AKERON MP fired from Patrol Boat.

sub-items.label: MBDA_SIAE23_02-08
Text
sub-items.text01:
Future Cruise / Anti-Ship Weapon systems

The next generation weapons for maritime superiority, counter-Anti Access/Area Denial and Deep Strike capabilities. STRATUS provides air and naval forces with operational advantage and freedom of action in the most complex and contested battlespace of the 2030s and beyond. STRATUS is a complementary and adaptable family of high speed and low observable long range precision strike cruise missiles. Based on decades of engineering excellence and know-how in subsonic and supersonic long range weapon systems, and equipped with state-of-the-art technical features, STRATUS will address modern and future Integrated Air Defense Systems, combat ships and high value targets deep inside enemy territory, in all weathers, and with a unrivalled degree of confidence and survivability. Platforms Integration on current and future air and naval platforms, with provision for ground-launched applications. Integration into future battlespace networks to be part of a wider system of systems capability.
